question: Nick and Alex worked on a paper together. If Nick wrote 2/7 of the paper and Alex wrote 20 pages, then what was the total length of the paper?

solution.strategy: Set up the equation based on the fraction of the paper written by Alex, which is 5/7 of the total pages, and equate it to 20 pages.
steps: Identify that 5/7 of the paper equals 20 pages.
Multiply both sides by 7 to eliminate the fraction: 5P = 140.
Divide both sides by 5 to solve for P: P = 28.

question: Maya and Ben worked on a report together. If Maya wrote 1/4 of the report and Ben wrote 27 pages, then what was the total length of the report?

solution.strategy: Identify the fraction Ben wrote (3/4) and set up an equation for the total.
steps: Ben wrote 3/4 of the report, so (3/4)P = 27.

question: Jenna and Kyle worked on a story together. If Jenna wrote 3/8 of the story and Kyle wrote 25 pages, then what was the total length of the story?

solution.strategy: Kyle wrote 5/8 of the story. Set up (5/8)P = 25 and solve for P.

solution.strategy: Dan wrote 3/5 of the project. Set up (3/5)P = 24 and solve for P.

solution.strategy: Sam wrote 2/3 of the essay. Set up (2/3)P = 16 and solve for P.
